reviewer_objection: Is the FCC row being overread as proof of broad deepfake deterrence or criminal liability?
bounded_response_now: No. The safe reading is procedural lifecycle closure only: public FCC materials support a bounded pathway from rule anchor to notice of apparent liability to consent-decree settlement with explicit compliance-plan terms and a USD 1000000 civil penalty. They do not support criminal-guilt, prevalence, or cross-domain deterrence claims.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: procedural-not-prevalence and civil-not-criminal boundaries remain explicit.

next_smallest_publishability_move: Only add origin-side or court-facing companion text if it becomes directly reachable.

reviewer_objection: Why are the Finland and Lithuania rows hidden from default figure and map views if rare-language coverage is a project priority?
bounded_response_now: Because current support for both rows remains below quote-safe substantive evidence. Finland has improved from bare title gloss to a metadata-recovered but body-unreadable queue state with a reproducible direct article URL plus public metadata, but it still lacks readable body text or an official companion source; Lithuania remains a mixed rights/access and unresolved-modality route. Prioritizing undercovered languages does not justify promoting machine-translated or barrier-heavy leads into substantive results.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: no legal or factual claim from translation, metadata recovery, or barrier-heavy route evidence alone.

reviewer_objection: Why keep the New Hampshire DOJ branch if the live host is blocked from this server?
bounded_response_now: Because the branch is no longer only title-visible negative evidence: public Wayback captures recover official New Hampshire DOJ charging-stage and investigation-update page text even though the live host still returns 403 from this server, and the recovered charging page now also relays to two text-resolved archived FCC NAL PDFs naming Kramer and Lingo Telecom at allegation stage. That makes the branch a useful archive-recovered origin-side reconstruction plus state-to-federal dossier row, while still falling short of a second results-grade closure package.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: no adjudication, no full chronology, and no claim that archive recovery plus archived FCC relay resolution alone makes the New Hampshire branch a second default-visible row.
